author: y4rr
content:
Phase 5 V3 batch 2: NVMe L3 tier wired into PagedLlamaInferencer + verified end-to-end on Llama 3 8B.

PagedLlamaInferencer::with_disk constructor + paged_llama3_8b example accepts PAGED_DISK_PATH and PAGED_HOST_MB. Fixed a real bug: spill_host_until_fits was refusing to drop VRAM-resident host shadows, leading to 'host budget too tight' errors. With disk as the authoritative tier, dropping the host shadow of a VRAM-resident block is safe — VRAM keeps its copy and disk has the data. Spill loop simplified to drop any LRU host block.

Run on Llama 3 8B with VRAM=4 GiB, Host=2 GiB, Disk=10.5 GiB:
* cos_sim = 0.998834 vs dense, argmax=311=311 (correctness preserved)
* All 21504 paged blocks land on disk
* Host holds 4096 blocks (2 GiB) at steady state
* VRAM stays at 4 GiB peak
* 21285 disk reads per forward, 10.4 GiB read per forward (entire paged set streams through)
* 3.80s/forward (vs 0.12s dense) — 30x slowdown, fully disk-bandwidth-bound at 99% block density
* This is the 70B-on-consumer-hardware scenario in miniature

74 tests pass, no warnings.

Remaining V3 axes (still pending, captured here as future work):
1. Generic store over T (Pod) — quant extensibility
2. Paged embed_tokens / lm_head — small VRAM win (~500 MiB on 8B)
3. Paged attention — head-level granularity, requires new attention sparsity predictor (orthogonal to MLP work)
4. KV cache + KV cache paging — major forward-pass refactor; currently re-runs from scratch each forward
5. Async DMA prefetch — cudarc multi-stream; overlaps fault-in with compute
6. internal tooling
